_meta.revenue_identity: Total Revenue = Solid Waste Revenue × (1 + price% + volume% + mix%) + Acquisition Revenue + Resource Solutions Revenue
sources: CWST Q4 FY2025 earnings press release (GlobeNewswire 2026-02-19): revenue $1.837B, EBITDA $422.8M, adj. FCF $179.9M
CWST Q1 2026 earnings press release (GlobeNewswire 2026-04-30): revenue $457.3M Q1, guidance raised to $2.06-2.08B FY2026
CWST FY2025 segment data: Eastern $472.6M, Western $663.2M, Mid-Atlantic $341.1M, Resource Solutions $360.0M
Pricing history: FY2023 +7.5%, FY2024 +5.7%, FY2025 +4.9%, Q1 2026 +5.1% (all solid waste, collection + disposal)
M&A: 9 deals in FY2025 (~$115M annualized), 4 in Q1 2026 (~$150M annualized incl. Star Waste $100M), $500M pipeline
2026 guidance: revenue $2.06-2.08B, EBITDA $473-483M, adj. FCF $200-210M, capex ~$260M
CWST 4-value-drivers article (Yahoo Finance): $150-200M near-term M&A pipeline, 70-75% volumes on open-market contracts
NEWMOA 2024 disposal capacity report: 33.56MT Northeast, 23% at sites closing in under 5 years; tipping fees $84.44/ton
Waste360 Feb 2026 industry report: pricing deceleration trends for WCN, GFL; CWST price-cost spread target 50-100bps
CWST databundle: FY2022-FY2025 financials (revenue, EBITDA, capex, FCF, balance sheet)

FY2023.note: Strong pricing year; GAAP FCF suppressed by high-growth capex

FY2024.note: Margin suppressed by Mid-Atlantic integration costs

FY2025.note: First year of meaningful margin improvement; EBITDA +170bps vs 2024

Q1_2026_annualized.note: Q1 seasonally weakest quarter; weather impact (-10% snowfall vs 10y avg); Q1 run-rate understates full-year

driver_1_organic_price.description: Solid waste pricing: blend of open-market (70-75% of volumes, repriced annually vs. market tipping fees) and CPI/WPI-indexed contracts (25-30%). The open-market component prices against Northeast tipping fee scarcity, which is structural not cyclical.
driver_1_organic_price.rationale: Base: Northeast capacity constraints maintain 5%+ disposal pricing; collection pricing tracks at similar level; management 2026 target explicitly 5%. Bull: tipping fees continue +$8-10/ton/year trajectory from Northeast closures (NEWMOA data), lifting disposal pricing to 6%+ and blending to 5.5%. Bear: economic softness and commercial volume decline force pricing discipline back toward CPI (now ~3-3.5%), pulling blended price growth to 4%.
driver_1_organic_price.source: CWST Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 press releases; Yahoo Finance 4-value-drivers; NEWMOA/EREF tipping fee data

driver_2_organic_volume.description: Organic volume (ex-acquisition) — residential and commercial collection route density, industrial/roll-off special waste. Flat-to-slightly-negative in recent quarters due to soft commercial construction.
driver_2_organic_volume.rationale: Base: Northeast residential volumes stable (GDP-correlated, ~0% organic over time for mature markets); commercial volumes recover modestly in 2027-2028 as interest rates normalize; special waste (C&D) remains soft. Bull: Northeast housing/construction recovery drives +1% route density uplift. Bear: recession-induced commercial volume decline of -1.5% offsets pricing gains.
driver_2_organic_volume.source: CWST FY2023-Q1 2026 earnings disclosures; Q1 2026 volume -2.1% (weather/economic); industry trend (RSG -1% volume Q1 2026)

driver_3_acquisition_revenue.description: M&A-driven revenue addition: CWST targets $100-200M of acquired annualized revenue per year from a $500M identified pipeline. Acquisitions are typically collection-only haulers in existing Northeast/Mid-Atlantic markets, priced at 7-9x pre-synergy EBITDA.
driver_3_acquisition_revenue.rationale: Base: $100M/year of closed acquisitions continues through 2028 (below the $150M 2026 pace which included the large Star Waste deal), tapering as the Northeast/Mid-Atlantic market consolidates. Pipeline is real — management cites $500M identified — but assumes deal flow moderates as the easiest targets get absorbed. Bull: $150M/year maintained through 2028, pipeline replenished by WV and adjacent market expansion (Mountain State Waste opened WV). Bear: capital markets tighten, leverage approaches 3x, management pauses M&A; $50M/year minimum driven by small bolt-ons.
driver_3_acquisition_revenue.source: CWST Q4 2025 press release (9 deals, $115M); Q1 2026 ($150M annualized, Star Waste $100M); Yahoo Finance 4-value-drivers ($500M pipeline); GlobeNewswire Q1 2026 guidance update

driver_4_internalization_lift.description: As acquired collection routes route waste to CWST-owned landfills instead of paying third-party tipping fees, both revenue (disposal) and margin improve. Internalized volumes grew 11% vs 3% for third-party in most recent disclosure — confirming the conversion pipeline.
driver_4_internalization_lift.current_implied_internalization_pct: est. 55-60% of solid waste collection volumes
driver_4_internalization_lift.margin_impact: Each 500bps of internalization improvement worth est. 50-75bps of blended EBITDA margin over 2-3 years
driver_4_internalization_lift.constraint: Constrained by Highland/Hakes permit approvals and Ontario County closure transition through 2028
driver_4_internalization_lift.source: Yahoo Finance 4-value-drivers article (internalized volumes +11%); CWST permit announcements (Highland, Hakes); CWST highland 460k to 1M ton expansion

driver_5_resource_solutions.description: Resource Solutions (~$360M in FY2025, ~20% of total revenue): recycling processing, organics (composting, anaerobic digestion), national accounts. Revenue driven by processing fees + commodity sales. Commodity exposure partially mitigated by risk-sharing contracts.
driver_5_resource_solutions.growth_outlook: 5-7% annually: national accounts growth is the driver; recycling volumes expand with regulatory mandates for material diversion; commodity pricing a wildcard (Q1 2026: -22% per ton YoY)
driver_5_resource_solutions.margin_profile: Lower margin than solid waste; processing fees provide floor; commodity revenue volatile. Risk management programs share downside with customers.
driver_5_resource_solutions.source: CWST FY2025 segment data; Q1 2026 earnings: RS revenue $88.8M (+%), commodity per ton -22% YoY

revenue_projections.methodology: Total revenue = Solid Waste Revenue × (1 + price + volume) + Acquisition Revenue Cumulative + Resource Solutions × (1 + growth). Solid Waste Revenue is the sum of Eastern + Western + Mid-Atlantic regional segments. FY2025 solid waste revenue: $1.837B - $0.360B RS = ~$1.477B.

FY2026.assumption_note: Base anchored to management guidance (raised): $2.06-2.08B revenue, $473-483M EBITDA. Solid waste pricing +5%, volume flat-to-slightly-negative ex-weather, $120M acquisition revenue already closed. Resource Solutions +5% from national accounts.
FY2026.assumption_sources: CWST Q1 2026 guidance raise (GlobeNewswire Apr 2026); Star Waste $100M annualized (closed Apr 1 2026)

FY2027.assumption_note: Base: solid waste pricing +5%, volume 0%, $100M additional acquisitions closed in 2027. Mid-Atlantic integration G&A savings realizing ($8-10M of $15M target). Highland permit expected Q1 2027 — internalization begins improving in H2 2027. EBITDA margin +110bps from 2026 as synergies accrue and SG&A deleverages on higher revenue base.
FY2027.assumption_sources: Management 2027 synergy commentary (above-trend margin improvement weighted to H2 2026 and 2027); Highland permit Q1 2027 expected; G&A at 12% of revenue target (CFO Q1 2026 call)

FY2028.assumption_note: Base: Ontario County closes end-2028 (EBITDA-neutral per guidance); Highland operating at expanded capacity (460k to 1M tons), improving internalization to ~62-65%. Full $15M G&A savings realizing. Pricing moderates to +4.5% as CPI eases but Northeast structural scarcity maintains floor. Revenue ceiling check: $2.4B / ~$1.48B solid waste base in 2025 implies a 4.5-year CAGR of ~7-8% total, plausible given $100-150M/year acquisitions plus organic 4-5%.
FY2028.implied_driver_check: Requires Northeast solid waste market to grow at ~3% annually (volume+price-mix in the $30-35B regional market) and CWST to hold ~7-8% share — entirely within reach given current $1.5B solid waste revenue and ~$20-25B addressable Northeast market
FY2028.assumption_sources: CWST crux.json base estimate; management Ontario guidance; Highland 1M ton capacity expansion; NEWMOA Northeast market size; EBITDA margin floor/ceiling in crux.json

FY2029.assumption_note: Base: acquisition pace normalizes to $75-100M/year as pipeline consolidates. Organic solid waste pricing +4% (CPI moderation in late-cycle but Northeast scarcity floor). Resource Solutions grows 5% from organics mandates.
FY2029.assumption_sources: Extrapolation from base drivers; acquisition pipeline moderation as Northeast market consolidates

FY2030_normalization.normalization_rationale: By 2030, CWST is in a steady-state: acquisition cadence drops to $50-75M/year (market consolidated), integration costs behind it, Highland/Hakes/McKean providing sufficient owned disposal, Ontario memory recedes. Margin at 27% base reflects CWST's trajectory toward but not yet at WCN/RSG levels, given ongoing M&A activity. This is the normalization year for exit-multiple calculation.
FY2030_normalization.assumption_sources: Crux.json base estimate 25.5% by 2028 extrapolated two more years of 80-90bps annual improvement; ceiling set by WCN 33%, RSG 32% with CWST discount for higher acquisition pace; floor set by current 23% plus minimum $1-2/year margin improvement

implied_driver_ceiling_check.check_name: Northeast solid waste TAM vs. CWST revenue path
implied_driver_ceiling_check.northeast_tam_estimate_b: 30-35 (estimated; 33.56M tons at $84-90/ton average blended + transfer/collection layers = ~$30-35B gross waste services market value)
implied_driver_ceiling_check.cwst_addressable_share_current_pct: 5-7 (CWST ~$1.5B solid waste revenue against ~$20-25B slice of the Northeast market that is addressable for a regional operator with CWST's footprint)
implied_driver_ceiling_check.cwst_implied_share_2030_base_pct: 8-10 (CWST ~$2.7B total, solid waste ~$2.0B, against a ~$22-25B addressable market growing at ~3-4%/year)
implied_driver_ceiling_check.verdict: PASS — CWST's 2030 base revenue does not imply an impossible market share. Even the bull at $3.0B implies ~11-12% share of the addressable Northeast/Mid-Atlantic market, still well within the range of a dominant regional operator. No ceiling breach.
implied_driver_ceiling_check.source: NEWMOA 33.56MT Northeast disposal volumes; tipping fee $84.44/ton (EREF 2024); CWST FY2025 segment geography; North America waste market $118B (Mordor Intelligence, 2026)

adj_fcf_path.note: Adjusted FCF (management definition excludes acquisition capex) vs. GAAP FCF diverge meaningfully. For valuation, GAAP FCF is the conservative measure; adjusted FCF is the management KPI. Both shown below.

FY2028E_base.gaap_fcf_b: est. 0.150-0.180 (capex normalizes to ~$200M as growth projects complete)

adj_fcf_path.capex_intensity_trend: Capex of 13.4% of revenue in FY2025 (including McKean rail, landfill cell construction). Expected to normalize toward 11-12% by 2028 as growth infrastructure projects complete, supporting improving conversion.
adj_fcf_path.source: CWST databundle: CFO $330M, capex $246M, FCF $84M in FY2025; adj. FCF $179.9M per Q4 2025 press release; 2026 capex guided ~$260M

Eastern.nature: Mature annuity — Vermont, New Hampshire, Maine legacy markets. Highest internalization rate, oldest routes, lowest growth. Pricing +4-5%/year. Volume flat to slightly positive.
Eastern.growth_2026_2030: 4-5% annual (pricing-led); minimal acquisition contribution (market largely consolidated); contribution to margin improvement via high internalization of disposal

Western.nature: Core growth engine — New York upstate, northern Pennsylvania. Benefits most from landfill expansion (Highland, Hakes, Hyland all in this geography). Highest disposal revenue concentration.
Western.growth_2026_2030: 6-8% annual base (pricing + acquisition fill + Highland capacity opening); margin expands most sharply here as Highland goes from 460k to 1M tons
Western.source: CWST FY2025 segment release; CWST permit announcements (Highland NY, Hakes NY)

Mid_Atlantic.nature: High growth, integration phase — Maryland, Pennsylvania (lower), New Jersey, Virginia. Acquisitions drove >15% revenue growth in FY2024. Integration of customer migrations into CWST systems is key; $15M G&A savings mainly concentrated here.
Mid_Atlantic.growth_2026_2030: 8-12% near-term (integration plus acquisitions); moderates to 5-6% by 2029 as market matures; margin improves from 2026-2028 as G&A savings and internalization kick in; McKean (1.6M tons) serves this region
Mid_Atlantic.source: CWST FY2025 segment release; StockStory Q3 2025 Mid-Atlantic commentary; Yahoo Finance 4-value-drivers G&A savings

Resource_Solutions.nature: Lower-margin, commodity-exposed — recycling MRFs, organics processing, national accounts. Revenue volatile with commodity cycles but processing fees provide a floor.
Resource_Solutions.growth_2026_2030: 5% base (national accounts growth + organics mandates offset commodity headwinds); Q1 2026 -22% commodity per ton is a near-term headwind; risk-sharing contracts limit downside
Resource_Solutions.source: CWST FY2025 and Q1 2026 earnings; GuruFocus Q1 2026 highlights
